articles.title: Claude Guillemot, one of Ubisoft's co-founders, has died in a plane crash
articles.why: Claude Guillemot is one of the five brothers who co-founded Ubisoft — one of the most important game companies ever built — and his death in a plane crash is a genuine loss for the industry that created Assassin's Creed, Far Cry, and Rainbow Six. This is a major figure in gaming history.
articles.hook: One of Ubisoft's five founding brothers died in a plane crash near the French coast — he helped build one of the most influential game companies in history.

articles.title: Drake Shares 'Endless and Eternal Gratitude' to Late Producer Tay Keith in Touching Tribute
articles.why: Tay Keith's death is already in the recently covered list, but Drake's public tribute adds a human layer to the loss — and given that 'Sicko Mode' remains one of the defining hip-hop collaborations of the decade, this moment has cultural weight beyond the news peg.
articles.hook: Drake posted a full Instagram tribute to Tay Keith, calling the producer behind 'Sicko Mode' irreplaceable — and it's the most personal thing he's shared publicly in months.

articles.title: Sexyy Red Declares Her and Tay Keith 'Da Best Duo' in Tribute While Fans Point to Label’s Lawsuit
articles.why: Sexyy Red's tribute to Tay Keith — while fans simultaneously surface a label lawsuit against him — captures the complicated, unresolved nature of his legacy in real time, adding a new dimension to an already significant story.
articles.hook: While Sexyy Red was calling her and Tay Keith the best duo, fans were pointing out his label had filed a lawsuit against him just before he died.

articles.title: James Bradley Dies: ‘Flags Of Our Fathers’ Co-Author Was 72
articles.why: James Bradley's Flags of Our Fathers is one of the most celebrated works of American WWII nonfiction — a book that reshaped how a generation understood Iwo Jima — and his death at 72 closes a significant chapter in that story.
articles.hook: James Bradley, who wrote Flags of Our Fathers after discovering his own father was one of the men who raised the flag on Iwo Jima, has died at 72.

articles.title: What Comes Next for Iran
articles.why: The US-Iran deal is the most consequential geopolitical event of the year, and a Washington Week panel — produced with The Atlantic — offers a rare, digestible breakdown of what comes next for the region, the deal's durability, and what America actually got in return.
articles.hook: The US-Iran ceasefire is signed, and the first serious question is whether Iran's internal politics will let it keep the deal it just got.

articles.title: Drake Pays Tribute to Tay Keith After Producer’s Death: ‘Endless and Eternal Gratitude’
articles.why: Drake publicly mourning Tay Keith — one of his most frequent collaborators — adds significant cultural weight to an already major loss. When one of the biggest artists alive stops to pay tribute, it confirms the magnitude of the moment for hip-hop.
articles.hook: Drake called Tay Keith's contributions to his music 'endless and eternal' in a tribute after the producer died at 29.

articles.title: Tay Keith’s Family Pays Tribute to Late Producer: A ‘Cultural Force’ & ‘Source of Love, Strength, Laughter, Guidance’
articles.why: Tay Keith's family releasing a formal statement after his death at 29 gives human grounding to what has become one of the most mourned losses in hip-hop production in years — the family's words are the definitive close on a week of tributes from across the industry.
articles.hook: Tay Keith's family called him 'a cultural force' and 'a source of love, strength, laughter, and guidance' in their first statement since his death.

articles.title: The riskiest SpaceX stock trade of all had a big first week
articles.why: SpaceX's IPO didn't just create a trillionaire — it triggered a historic land grab in leveraged ETFs, meaning retail investors are betting on SpaceX with amplified risk they may not fully understand. The first week's data reveals how speculative the frenzy has become.
articles.hook: SpaceX's IPO triggered one of the biggest leveraged ETF land grabs in market history, meaning millions of retail investors are now holding amplified bets on a company three times more volatile than bitcoin.

articles.title: 10 reasons why the World Cup is the greatest sporting event on Earth
articles.why: A well-argued cultural case for why the World Cup uniquely earns its 'greatest sporting event' billing — timely, well-sourced, and pitched at the exact moment global audiences are watching together.
articles.hook: The World Cup is the only major sporting event that actually delivers on its name — and this piece makes the case in ten specific, surprising reasons.

articles.title: All the Eerie Parallels Between Earth, Wind & Fire and Chicago
articles.why: The structural parallels between Earth, Wind & Fire and Chicago — same city, same era, same horn section, same producer, same arc of decline — is the kind of music history deep-dive that genuinely reframes how you hear both bands.
articles.hook: Earth, Wind & Fire and Chicago both came out of Chicago in the late '60s, both had horn sections, both reluctantly went disco, and both hired the same producer — the parallels are almost eerie.

articles.title: Toy Story has the right take on tech
articles.why: A thoughtful editorial connecting Toy Story 5's anti-tech villain to the broader moment in consumer tech — arriving the same week new Siri launches and Snap ships AR glasses — makes this more than a film recap.
articles.hook: The week's biggest Pixar release uses a tablet as its villain, and the timing with new Siri and Snap's AR glasses is almost too perfect.
